hero_subtitle: SFTY connects multi-provider Travel Rule outputs into structured cases that streamline exception handling, preserve audit evidence and support regulatory review for VASPs.

problem_title: Travel Rule data remains disconnected from cases
problem_description: Travel Rule messages and exception details often sit outside structured case workflows, creating gaps in compliance reviews. This separation slows operational response and weakens traceability between message routing, risk assessment and case evidence for VASPs.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates Travel Rule message data, exception details and provider outputs into one case management workflow so VASPs can streamline reviews, preserve audit evidence and maintain operational traceability.
workflow_title: From Travel Rule exceptions to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY routes Travel Rule messages and exceptions through status tracking, case creation, evidence preservation and structured reporting within one operational workflow.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit

why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for Travel Rule workflows?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects Travel Rule outputs into structured workflows, routing messages, tracking status, handling exceptions and linking relevant data for comprehensive case and compliance management.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY manage Travel Rule cases?
faq_2_answer: SFTY creates cases from failed, high-risk or incomplete Travel Rule events, routes them for review and tracks decisions while maintaining audit logs and evidence packages.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve Travel Rule evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves Travel Rule audit evidence by recording message histories, decisions, reviewer notes and linked transaction data to support internal and regulatory reviews.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY sits above Travel Rule providers, connecting their outputs into one compliance layer without disrupting existing provider relationships or operations.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace Travel Rule providers but complements them by orchestrating outputs into connected workflows, case management and audit-ready reporting.
final_title: Connect Travel Rule outputs into structured cases
final_description: Review how SFTY links multi-provider Travel Rule data into cases, preserving audit evidence and supporting controlled operational workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
